everyone.name: Paula Kennedy
everyone.bio: Paula is part of the organising committee for Kubernetes Community Days UK, DevOpsDays London, FastFlowConf, and the London Platform User Group. In her day job, Paula is Co-Founder and Chief Operating Officer of Syntasso. She is passionate about community, diversity and inclusion, enjoys speaking at events like KubeCon and also cohosted the first Platform Engineering colocated day at KubeCon Paris. In her spare time, Paula enjoys walking her dog and attempting to run half-marathons.

everyone.name: Matteo Mortari
everyone.bio: I strongly believe Kubernetes is an awesome platform not only for cloud-native, app orchestration and workload, but especially for MLOps (Kubeflow) and in Containerization: not only for runnable images but also for broad OCI packaging and distribution (ORAS/Podman/Buildah) especially in relation to ML model assets and integration with signature, attestation. I contribute to Kubeflow, Oras, and other Open Source projects. I'm a co-organizer of the local JUG (JVM User Group) Milano, and I'm active in other Italian communities, often contributing my passion/hobby of live streaming and video recording.

everyone.name: Matthias Loibl
everyone.bio: Matthias is the Director of PolarSignals Cloud. He maintains Prometheus, the Prometheus Operator, Parca, Pyrra, and more. He is an emeritus Thanos maintainer. He organized PromCon (the Prometheus conference) in 2023 and 2024. He runs the Berlin Prometheus MeetUp.

everyone.name: Jana Vonšák
everyone.bio: DevOps Security Engineer fascinated by the tech world 🤖. Fueled by coffee ☕. Hitting the gym in my free time 💪🏻. Here to grab a fancy jacket 🌞

everyone.name: Vivek Ragupathy
everyone.bio: Experienced Site Reliability Engineer & Cloud Architect specializing in Kubernetes, DevSecOps, and cloud automation. With over 12 years of experience, I design and manage scalable, high-availability infrastructure, leveraging Terraform, Ansible, Docker, and Kubernetes to enhance reliability and security. Passionate about open-source technologies, I focus on automation frameworks, large-scale deployments, and observability with Prometheus, Datadog, and Elasticsearch. Dedicated to continuous learning, collaboration, and solving complex infrastructure challenges with innovative solutions.

ambassadors.name: Paula Kennedy
ambassadors.bio: Paula is part of the organising committee for Kubernetes Community Days UK, DevOpsDays London, FastFlowConf, and the London Platform User Group. In her day job, Paula is Co-Founder and Chief Operating Officer of Syntasso. She is passionate about community, diversity and inclusion, enjoys speaking at events like KubeCon and also cohosted the first Platform Engineering colocated day at KubeCon Paris. In her spare time, Paula enjoys walking her dog and attempting to run half-marathons.

ambassadors.name: Puja Abbassi
ambassadors.bio: Puja Abbassi is the Vice President of Product at Giant Swarm, building a managed cloud native developer platform based on Kubernetes. In Kubernetes he focuses on extending Kubernetes with custom resources and controllers. With many years of Kubernetes experience and having been in the beta batch of CKAs, he enjoys solving problems and helping people in the community, mainly within SIG Contributor Experience. He used to also be a contributor to the CIS Kubernetes Benchmarks. I blog at https://www.giantswarm.io/blog/author/puja-abbassi and sometimes on the CNCF or other community blogs.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: As a CNCF ambassador, I'm passionate about bringing cloud native technologies to more developers and their organisations around the globe. Especially, as the community is growing both from a project perspective as well as on the end user side, I'm excited to bring the two closer together and see how we can enable end user organisations to build cloud native platforms using CNCF projects.Sessionize Profile: https://sessionize.com/puja

ambassadors.name: Afzal Ansari
ambassadors.bio: Afzal is a passionate open-source enthusiast with a deep interest in cloud-native technologies and building new things on top of them. He specializes in supporting cloud infrastructure and actively contributes to ArgoCD open-source project. Additionally, he has made significant contributions to various CNCF projects, including Meshery, Keptn, and Jaeger. His dedication to the Keptn project earned him recognition as a Keptn Crew member. He also enjoys creating video content and writing blogs to share his experiences in the cloud-native space with like-minded individuals.

ambassadors.name: Shane Wang
ambassadors.bio: Dr. Shane Wang is an engineering director of Cloud Native China at Software and Advanced Technology Group in Intel. He and his team have been working on open source cloud software for many years since 2012, including Kubernetes, Istio, Envoy, containerd, serverless, edge, Ceph and OpenStack etc. He is an individual director of OIF foundation, alliance committee chair of SODA foundation, a TOC member of Mulan open source community, and one of the standing committee members of Open Source Development Committee in China Computer Federation (CCF), and one of Tencent Cloud Valuable Professionals. He organizes and runs cloud native meetups with China partners primarily in Shanghai and Beijing, focusing on Kubernetes, Istio and Envoy technologies.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Because with that I can organize regular meetups better in China with the local community including Tetrate, DaoCloud, Alauda, Alibaba and Tencent etc. bridging between CNCF and many China-based open source communities, I can drive more engineering contributions to CNCF projects and engagements with China customers for adoption. I'm also able to contribute more technical materials to CNCF Webinars and Blogs, etc. and pursue more presentation opportunities at CNCF conferences e.g. KubeCon China.

ambassadors.name: Adam Gardner
ambassadors.bio: Adam is part of the Open-Source Program Office (OSPO) at DT where he works as a DevRel focusing on observability and security. SCUBA diving is his passion. Outside work you can find him on, in or under the sea.

ambassadors.name: Faeka Ansari
ambassadors.bio: Faeka dove into the world of cloud-native projects during her student days, and the thrill of it has kept her hooked ever since. Originally drawn in by the technology, she quickly discovered that it was the people in the community who made it truly special

ambassadors.name: Huan Wei
ambassadors.bio: Huan has more than 15 years of work experience, and his current focus is cloud-native edge computing, distributed cloud, and high-performance computing. He is currently working as a Senior Technical Director at HarmonyCloud, a company located in Hangzhou, China, providing leading cloud-native products and solutions for hundreds of enterprise customers, including Kubernetes, PaaS, edge computing, micro service visibility, DevOps, etc. In addition, Huan serves as a Technical Steering Committe member of the CNCF edge computing project KubeEdge. Huan is an open source technology enthusiast and is currently committed to building an open source technology ecosystem in China.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: The company I work for, HarmonyCloud, is an influential cloud native tehnology supplier in China and a long-term partner of CNCF. HarmonyCloud has helped many customers around the world complete the transformation of cloud-native technology. At the same time, I am currently working as a TSC member in the CNCF Project KubeEdge community, responsible for leading several SIG teams and working with other TSC members towards the graduation of the KubeEdge project. Over the years, I have also given speeches at conferences organized by CNCF. Therefore, I am familiar with the operation process of CNCF projects and understand the job responsibilities of CNCF ambassadors. If I become an ambassador, I will promote the maturity and promotion of Kubernetes, KubeEdge and more open source projects, and help users around the world to apply these advanced and open source cloud-native tenologies.

ambassadors.name: Nancy Chauhan
ambassadors.bio: I like hacking through software engineering problems. Over the past 3-4 years, I have been developing solutions for software reliability. I love everything around cloud-native, open source, and security. I have also founded Women in Cloud Native community for women who are interested in learning more about Cloud Native and those who are willing to learn, collaborate and support each other to grow Website - https://womenincloudnative.com/ Personal Blog - https://nancychauhan.in/Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I look forward to contributing to the CNCF community as an ambassador and keep doing things I was doing before. Meeting other like-minded ambassadors and motivating each other to contribute, support, and inspire others excites me

kubestronauts.name: Jana Vonšák
kubestronauts.bio: DevOps Security Engineer fascinated by the tech world 🤖. Fueled by coffee ☕. Hitting the gym in my free time 💪🏻. Here to grab a fancy jacket 🌞

kubestronauts.name: Lauri Tirkkonen
kubestronauts.bio: I'm an independent consultant with specialties in Linux systems engineering and cloud architecture. Always open to new opportunities, so feel free to reach out!

kubestronauts.name: Naga Priyanka Kanna
kubestronauts.bio: I am a DevOps Engineer and cloud enthusiast with 10+ years of IT experience, including 4 years working with Azure, Kubernetes, Helm, Prometheus, Grafana, Jenkins, and CI/CD pipelines. I began my career with Tata Consultancy Services and later took a parenting career break to care for my newborn and toddler, while continuing to upskill in CNCF and DevOps technologies—even during sleepless nights. I actively share my learnings through LinkedIn articles on DevOps tools and proudly earned the Kubestronaut badge as the first female in Finland, a special achievement that reflects my commitment, hard work, and dedication to continuous improvement. I am now ready to re-enter the workforce with renewed energy, resilience, and passion for cloud-native technologies.

kubestronauts.name: Hogan Yan
kubestronauts.bio: Passionate software engineer who loves building cool stuff!

goldenKubestronauts.name: Lavanya Anbalagan
goldenKubestronauts.bio: I am an enthusiastic learner with over five years of experience in enterprise container platforms and holds a Kubestronaut title. In addition to my professional achievements, I have a personal interest in exploring new places and maintaining physical fitness.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Andreas Døhl
goldenKubestronauts.bio: Cloud security consultant from Norway focused on Kubernetes security, policy enforcement, and runtime hardening. I advise teams on how to build secure, scalable platforms — from pipelines to production clusters.

goldenKubestronauts.name: V-Ris Jaijongrak
goldenKubestronauts.bio: A Developer turned into DevOps culture. Enthusiastic towards Cloud Computing Technology and loves to learn new stuffs. Now focuses on adapting Security practices on CI/CD pipelines and GitOps

goldenKubestronauts.name: Andrei Rusnac
goldenKubestronauts.bio: Coming from the Networking and Linux field I am a DevOps engineer who loves spending time discovering new things and troubleshooting infrastructures. I like to research new methods of optimizing products and to find extravagant ways to solve the unsolvable.
